summary: Phase A frontend e2e tests passed 100%. Verified: page title, 4 presets (light/standard/aggressive/stealth), stealth highlighted with fuchsia border + ShieldCheck icon, all Phase A toggles (pitchtime/resamplechain/tape/pinknoise) visible, preset-stealth turns ALL Phase A toggles ON, preset-light turns ALL OFF, conditional sliders (slider-ratepct, slider-drive, slider-pinklevel) appear/hide correctly. Full E2E with 3s 440Hz mono WAV: upload -> stealth preset -> process -> waveform-processed + audio-processed both rendered (audio readyState=4, no error) -> download produced 't_clean.wav'. Zero console errors. Zero /api/* calls. Mobile (390x844) preset grid uses grid-cols-2 (2x2 wrap). Output duration differed (input 0:03 -> cleaned 0:02) due to playbackRate>1 in stealth — handled cleanly by OfflineAudioContext at 47983 Hz intermediate rate without errors.
critical_code_review_comments: audioProcessor.js processBuffer pass1 length formula `Math.ceil((audioBuffer.length * (passRate/origRate)) / playbackRate)` is correct, but if user disables resampleChain while pitchTime is enabled, output length differs from original — handled gracefully but worth documenting in UI.

Pink noise & dither nodes connect directly to offline.destination instead of post-saturation node; this means filters/saturation do NOT shape the injected noise. This is by design (noise should be flat/pink at output), but the comment in code could clarify that the noise is added post-FX.
Files remain well under 700 LOC; SettingsPanel ~250 lines, audioProcessor ~280 lines — clean separation.

Minor: `preset-stealth` border uses fuchsia for active state but inactive stealth still shows pale fuchsia ShieldCheck — accessibility-wise, ensure the icon contrast is sufficient on dark bg; visually OK in screenshots.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pure static SPA at https://music-no-mark.preview.emergentagent.com (no backend). Phase A added 4 new toggles + 3 new conditional sliders (testids: toggle-pitchtime, toggle-resamplechain, toggle-tape, toggle-pinknoise; slider-ratepct, slider-drive, slider-pinklevel) and a 4th preset 'stealth' (fuchsia theme with ShieldCheck icon). Two-pass processing via OfflineAudioContext at 47983 Hz then back to 44100 Hz works on Chromium. Output duration may differ from input due to playbackRate (pitch+time shift) — this is expected behavior, not a bug. Use 3+ second WAV (`python3 -c "import wave,struct,math;sr=44100;f=wave.open('/tmp/t.wav','wb');f.setnchannels(1);f.setsampwidth(2);f.setframerate(sr);[f.writeframes(struct.pack('<h',int(32767*0.3*math.sin(2*math.pi*440*i/sr)))) for i in range(sr*3)];f.close()"`) so the OfflineAudioContext load is non-trivial. Download filename pattern is '{originalBasename}_clean.wav'.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found in Phase A. The risky path (renderPass with playbackRate != 1 + non-44100 sampleRate=47983 + buffer source from a pass-1 AudioBuffer) renders without OfflineAudioContext constructor errors and produces a valid playable cleaned WAV.
